quiz1.missionText: Check what events are scheduled next month
quiz1.failureReason: Swipe left on the calendar to show next month

insight.title: How did you check next month?
insight.subtitle: Horizontal swipe switches months intuitively
insight.swipeTitle: Horizontal swipe is the standard page-turn gesture
insight.swipeDesc: In calendar apps, swiping left goes to the next month and swiping right goes to the previous month. This is consistent across almost all calendar apps.
insight.calendarTitle: Calendars are managed month by month
insight.calendarDesc: Smartphone calendar apps switch months by swiping rather than continuous scrolling. This month-by-month swipe UI design has become the mainstream.
insight.gestureTitle: You can operate even hidden UI
insight.gestureDesc: Even without a 'Next Month' button on screen, you can navigate using swipe gestures. This is one of the defining features of smartphone UI design.

quiz2.missionText: Add a new event on the 15th
quiz2.failureReason: Long press the 15th cell to add an event

insight.title: How could you add by long pressing?
insight.subtitle: Long press reveals hidden action menus
insight.longPressTitle: Long press opens a context menu
insight.longPressDesc: Long pressing a calendar date lets you directly add an event for that day. This is a hidden but convenient feature adopted by many calendar apps.
insight.fabTitle: The + button is the universal language for creation
insight.fabDesc: The + (plus) button in the bottom right is a UI pattern that means 'create something new', which has become a world standard centered around Google apps.
insight.addDesc: Adding an event to the calendar can be done in two ways: 'long press a date' or 'tap the + button'. Knowing both expands your operational choices.

quiz3.failureReason: Long press the event and drag & drop it to the next day

insight.title: How did you move the event to the next day?
insight.subtitle: Drag & drop is intuitive for moving events
insight.tapTitle: Long press and drag to move events
insight.tapDesc: Long pressing an event lets you drag it to a new date. This works for rescheduling existing events to different days.
insight.futureTitle: You can reschedule events to future dates
insight.futureDesc: Smartphone calendars let you move events to any date — days, weeks, or months ahead. Adjusting schedules is key to effective time management.
insight.planDesc: Moving what you need to do to the next day lets you adjust plans as needed. The ability to reschedule events helps you stay organized amidst changes.

quiz4.failureReason: Tap the calendar icon (Today) button in the AppBar

insight.title: How could you return to 'today' so quickly?
insight.subtitle: The 'Today' button saves you from getting lost
insight.todayTitle: 'Today' button is an essential calendar feature
insight.todayDesc: Calendar apps have a dedicated button to jump to today's date. Even after navigating far into the future or past, you can return instantly.
insight.iconDesc: The calendar icon displayed in the AppBar means 'show today's date'. This is a typical example of communicating function through icon design.
insight.jumpTitle: Jumping is more efficient than scrolling
insight.jumpDesc: When returning from years in the future or past to the present, jumping directly with a dedicated button is overwhelmingly faster than swiping back one month at a time.
